summary: sam-package exfiltrates @tines/apps endpoint response to attacker webhook on require
details: On `require('sam-package')`, index.js unconditionally invokes a function literally named `exploit()` which calls `invokeEndpoint('cce8dd37-...')` from `@tines/apps` and POSTs the response (or error) to a hardcoded webhook at https://bin.webhookrelay.com/v1/webhooks/f982048d-7436-4b00-b508-89f5eb657645. The README documents only a `greet()` helper; the import-time exploit behavior is undocumented. Any installer that requires this package will silently invoke a Tines endpoint using the ambient auth/identity of the install context and forward the result to an attacker-controlled webhook relay. The naming, hidden execution path, and opaque relay destination are consistent with a dependency-confusion proof-of-concept or token-harvest payload, not a legitimate greeter library.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
